titles.en: Reward of reciting Azaan in a Muslim city for a year

text: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ مُصْعَبِ بْنِ سَلَّامٍ التَّیْمِیِّ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ ظَرِیفٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: مَنْ أَذَّنَ سَبْعَ سِنِینَ مُحْتَسِباً جَاءَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ لَا ذَنْبَ لَهُ.

en.sayed-athar-husain-rizvi-&-sayed-maqs: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ali from Mus’ab b. Salam al-Taymi from Sa’d b. Dharif from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: “Paradise becomes incumbent on one who recites Azaan for a year in a city having Muslim population.”
